sources.name: HPO ontology
sources.notes: Confirmed present as a 22MB asset on release v2026-06-23 via the GitHub releases asset listing. Direct byte-fetch of the release-asset URL itself was not confirmed by this tool (see narrative report note (a)); expected to work via update_data.py's plain urllib request.

sources.name: HPO disease annotations
sources.notes: Confirmed present as a 34MB asset on release v2026-06-23. Same caveat as hp.json above.

sources.name: HPO gene-disease associations
sources.notes: Confirmed present as a 1.41MB asset on release v2026-06-23. Same caveat as hp.json above.

sources.name: HGNC complete gene set
sources.notes: Direct fetch succeeded; real TSV content retrieved.

sources.name: Mondo to OMIM exact-match crosswalk
sources.notes: Direct fetch succeeded; 585 lines of real SSSOM content retrieved.

sources.name: Mondo full mapping set (Orphanet exact-match subset extracted by build_db.py)
sources.notes: Real file is 12.5MB (per GitHub's own file view); only the first ~78KB was retrievable through this tool's fetch size cap, but that partial content was valid SSSOM data, and update_data.py's plain urllib download has no such cap.

sources.name: ClinGen gene-disease validity summary
sources.notes: No stable, unauthenticated URL found (checked the interactive downloads page, the public FTP mirror, and the documents page). Manual download required each month.

deployment.notes: First fetch of the bare URL right after a push returned a stale cached page; a cache-busted fetch immediately showed the correct, up-to-date content. Documented in DEPLOYMENT.md.

known_gaps: hpo_source and hgnc_source meta fields are generic placeholder text this build, not an actual captured version string -- check whether a fresh update_data.py run resolves this.
ClinGen's two summary CSVs still require a manual download every month; no stable URL exists.

The three HPO GitHub release-asset URLs could not be independently byte-verified by this tool (empty response, but the same failure occurs for an unrelated repo's release asset too, indicating a tool limitation rather than a broken URL) -- confirm on first real update_data.py run.
